channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sindh Government Declares Holiday on April 4 to Commemorate Zulfikar Ali Bhutto
item.description: The Sindh provincial government has officially declared a public holiday on Saturday, April 4, in memory of former Prime Minister and Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP) founder Zulfikar Ali Bhutto. Accordin...

item.title: How Lost Crypto Keys Complicate Inheritance for Older Australians
item.description: A growing number of older Australians are investing in cryptocurrencies, but traditional estate planning systems are struggling to manage these digital assets after their owners pass away. The article...

item.title: Nyamira Governor Clarifies Only 13 Unclaimed Bodies Released for Burial Amid Investigation
item.description: Nyamira Governor Amos Nyaribo has publicly refuted reports claiming that 33 unclaimed bodies were released by his administration for burial in Kericho. He clarified that only 13 bodies were handed ove...

item.title: Two Lethbridge men face first-degree murder charges in death of 27-year-old man
item.description: Lethbridge Police have charged two men with first-degree murder in connection with the death of a 27-year-old man that occurred on March 27, 2026. Officers and EMS responded to a report of an assault ...

item.title: Over ₱651 Million in Fuel Subsidy Distributed to PUV Drivers and Operators, Says LTFRB
item.description: The Land Transportation Franchising and Regulatory Board (LTFRB) reported that more than ₱651 million has been distributed to public transport drivers and operators under the government’s fuel subsidy...

item.title: Tottenham Consider De Zerbi to Replace Tudor After Short Spell
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur don begin serious talk to bring Roberto De Zerbi come manage dem after Igor Tudor waka comot suddenly. The Italian coach, wey dey popular for him attacking football and skill to impr...

item.title: Air India Express flight safely lands in Lucknow after precautionary Mayday alert
item.description: On Monday evening, an Air India Express flight operating from Bagdogra to Delhi, flight IX 1523, was diverted to Lucknow following a Mayday call due to suspected smoke inside the cabin. While cruising...

item.title: Protests in South Africa Turn Violent Over Igbo King Coronation, Foreign-Owned Shops Targeted
item.description: For South Africa, tori don hot as protesters don scatter for Eastern Cape over the coronation of one Nigerian Igbo king. The wahala start when members of political group ActionSA, together with local ...

item.title: AIIMS INI CET 2026: Application Process for July Session Commences
item.description: The National Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS) has officially commenced the application process for the INI CET (All India Institute of Medical Sciences Integrated National Entrance Test) for the ...

item.title: Kenya-South Africa Initiative Could Revolutionize African Transportation with Electric Motorcycles
item.description: A recent article highlights the potential for electric motorcycles, manufactured locally and powered by solar energy, to transform transportation across Africa. The journey from Nairobi to Stellenbosc...

item.title: Documentary Highlights South Africa’s Unequal and Unsustainable Food System
item.description: A new documentary titled 'What Feeds Us' sheds light on the inequalities and environmental harm caused by South Africa’s industrialised agricultural system. The film, directed by Alex Hendricks, shows...

item.title: Supreme Court to Re-examine Escudero Campaign Donation Ruling
item.description: The Supreme Court has agreed to review a decision made by the Commission on Elections (Comelec) that cleared Senator Chiz Escudero of any wrongdoing regarding a P30-million campaign donation he receiv...

item.title: Fuel crisis in Australia severely impacts vanlifers and people living on the streets
item.description: Australia's ongoing fuel crisis is having a significant impact on vanlifers and vulnerable communities. For some, like Sharon, who planned a long road trip around the country in her customised van, so...

item.title: Police move to arrest Nairobi Governor Johnson Sakaja over contempt of Parliament
item.description: Police are actively seeking Nairobi Governor Johnson Sakaja following a directive from the Senate watchdog committee, which found him in contempt of Parliament after he ignored multiple summons. The C...

item.title: Legal dispute threatens to delay South Africa’s foot-and-mouth vaccination programme
item.description: The Red Meat Producers Organisation (RPO) has raised concerns that legal action by veterinary bodies could stall the rollout of the foot-and-mouth disease (FMD) vaccination campaign in South Africa, a...

item.title: U.S. Markets Edge Higher as Oil Tops $100 a Barrel, Energy Stocks Lead Gains
item.description: U.S. stock markets rose cautiously on Monday, reflecting both optimism and unease as energy prices continued to surge. The S&P 500, Dow Jones Industrial Average, and Nasdaq Composite all posted modest...

item.title: Delhi Government Plans Expansion of Piped Gas Network to 4 Lakh Additional Homes
item.description: The Delhi government is making a major push to expand the city’s piped natural gas (PNG) network by targeting about four lakh new connections in the coming financial year. This initiative is part of a...

item.title: Arnold Schwarzenegger honoured with honorary doctorate at Ulster University
item.description: Arnold Schwarzenegger visited Belfast to receive an honorary doctorate from Ulster University, recognising his contributions to public service, environmental advocacy, and the arts. The 78-year-old ac...

item.title: Lake Burley Griffin partially closed due to suspected blue-green algae outbreak
item.description: An outbreak of suspected blue-green algae has led to the closure of parts of Lake Burley Griffin to swimmers. On Monday, a bright green substance was seen across the Central Basin, with ducks observed...

item.title: Severe Weather Alert: PMD Forecasts Heavy Rains, Thunderstorms Across Pakistan from April 1
item.description: The Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has issued a comprehensive weather advisory forecasting widespread rain, thunderstorms, and strong winds across most parts of the country from April 1 to 4...
